title: Compute a UART baud-rate divisor
description: Derive the integer divisor from the peripheral clock and target baud, then check the resulting error percentage.
body: Use when configuring a UART whose register takes a clock divisor rather than a baud value. Do not round the divisor without checking the resulting baud error, since large error breaks framing at long frames. Actions: divide the peripheral clock by the target baud and any oversampling factor, round to the nearest integer, and compute the actual baud error. Validation signals include a baud error below the tolerance for the frame length and a clean test-string receive. Risk boundary: a divisor error above a couple percent corrupts long frames, so escalate to a different clock source when the error exceeds tolerance.
